back_translation: Then Joshua son of Nun secretly sent two men from Shittim to spy out the land, saying, "Go and inspect the land, especially the city of Jericho." So they went and entered the house of a prostitute named Rahab, and they lodged there.
And it was reported to the king of Jericho: "Behold, some men of the descendants of Israel have come here tonight to spy out the land."
The king of Jericho therefore sent word to Rahab, saying, "Bring out the men who came to you and entered your house, for they have come to spy out the whole land."
But the woman had brought the two men in and already hidden them. So she answered, "Yes, those men did come to me, but I do not know where they came from.
About the time the city gate was about to close at dusk, the men went out. I do not know which way they went. Pursue them quickly; you may catch them."
(But Rahab had taken the two up onto the roof and hidden them under the stalks of flax which she had spread out on the roof.)
So the pursuers chased them along the road to the Jordan River, as far as the fords; and as soon as the pursuers had gone out of the city, the city gate was shut.
Before the two spies lay down to sleep, Rahab went up onto the roof to them
and said to them, "I know that the Lord has given this land to you-pl, and that the fear you-pl cause has fallen upon us, and all the inhabitants of the land are melting in heart before you-pl.

For we have heard that the Lord dried up the waters of the Red Sea before you-pl when you-pl came out of Egypt, and what you-pl did to the two kings of the Amorites east of the Jordan, namely Sihon and Og, whom you-pl devoted to utter destruction.
When we heard these things, our hearts melted, and no spirit was left in any man to rise up before you-pl; for the Lord your-pl God, He is God in the heavens above and on the earth below.
Therefore now, please swear to me by the Lord that because I have shown steadfast love to you-pl, you-pl will also show steadfast love to my father's household, and you-pl will give me a sure sign,
and that you-pl will spare my father and mother, my brothers and sisters, and all who belong to them, and will rescue us from death."
The two men answered her, "Our lives in exchange for yours! If you-pl do not report our matter, then when the Lord gives us the land, we will show you steadfast love and faithfulness."
Then Rahab let them down by a rope through the window, for her house was set in the wall of the city wall, and she dwelt in the wall.
She said to them, "Go to the hill country, so that the pursuers will not encounter you. Hide yourselves there for three days until the pursuers return; after that you may go on your way."
The men said to her, "We will be released from this oath you made us swear

unless, when we come into the land, you tie this scarlet cord at the window through which you let us down, and you gather your father and mother, your brothers, and the whole household of your father into your house.

Whoever goes out from the door of your house into the street, his blood will be on his own head, and we will be clear; but whoever is with you in the house, his blood will be on our heads if any hand touches him.
And if you report our matter, we will be released from the oath you made us swear."
She answered, "Let it be as your-pl words say." Then she sent them off; and when they had gone, she tied the scarlet cord in the window.
So they went out into the hill country and stayed there three days, until the pursuers returned. The pursuers searched all along the road but did not find them.
Then the two men started back, came down from the hill country, crossed the river, and came to Joshua son of Nun, and reported to him everything that had happened to them.
They said to Joshua, "Truly the Lord has given the entire land into our hands; moreover, all the inhabitants of the land are melting in heart before us."
